website_landing_markdown:
# Codex Account Switcher

A macOS menu bar app for people who run Codex heavily across multiple accounts.

## Why it exists
Switching accounts by editing `~/.codex/auth.json` manually is fragile and slow. This app keeps accounts ready and lets you switch active auth in one click.

## What you get
- One-click active account switch
- Account add/remove from menu bar
- 5-hour + weekly remaining usage bars per account
- Quick actions to restart Codex desktop and open a new CLI session

## Built for real Codex workflows
The app is focused on operator speed, clear state, and predictable switching behavior.

## Open source
- Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher
- Stack: SwiftUI + Swift Package Manager

## Notes
- Existing sessions do not hot-reload auth; restart/new session is required after switch.
- Credentials are stored locally with restrictive permissions.

## CTA
[View source on GitHub](https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher)
blog_post_markdown:
# Launch: Codex Account Switcher (Open Source)

I built **Codex Account Switcher**, a macOS menu bar utility for people running Codex across multiple accounts.

## The problem
If you use Codex desktop + CLI heavily, account switching usually means manually editing `~/.codex/auth.json`, restarting tools, and keeping usage windows in your head.

## What this tool changes
- Keep multiple accounts saved
- Switch active account with one click
- See remaining quota for 5-hour and weekly windows per account
- Trigger Codex app restart and new CLI session from the same menu

## Why open source
Auth/account tooling should be inspectable. The repository is public so you can audit behavior and adapt it to your own workflow.

## Current limitations
- Running sessions do not hot-reload auth
- Assumes file-backed auth (`~/.codex/auth.json`)

## Try it
```bash
git clone https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher.git
cd codex-account-switcher
swift run CodexAccountSwitcherApp
```

If you manage multiple Codex accounts daily, this should remove a lot of friction.

Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher

If you’re manually editing ~/.codex/auth.json to switch accounts, this might help. Codex Account Switcher gives one-click switching + usage bars per account in the menu bar. OSS: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher

reddit_posts.title: [Open Source] Codex Account Switcher for macOS menu bar
reddit_posts.body_markdown:
I built and open sourced a small macOS menu bar utility called **Codex Account Switcher**.

It’s for people using Codex heavily across multiple accounts and getting tired of manually editing `~/.codex/auth.json`.

What it does:
- Store multiple account profiles
- One-click active account switch
- Show remaining 5-hour + weekly usage windows per account
- Quick actions to restart Codex app / open new CLI session

Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher

Happy to share implementation details if useful.

reddit_posts.title: I made an OSS tool to switch Codex accounts from the menu bar
reddit_posts.body_markdown:
Launched a tiny OSS macOS utility: **Codex Account Switcher**.

It helps when you work across multiple Codex accounts and want faster switching + usage visibility without editing auth files manually.

Current focus is practical workflow speed (switch account, check 5h/weekly remaining, restart app/open CLI quickly).

Repo: https://github.com/naveenshaji/codex-account-switcher

Feedback welcome, especially from people running long Codex sessions daily.
